sections.paragraph:
ἀναπετάννυμι 1 aor. ptc. ἀναπετάσας Job 39:26; 1 aor. pass. ἀνεπετάσθην (πετάννυμι ‘spread out’; Hom. et al.; PMich 425, 16 [198 a.d.]; Job 39:26 [‘spread wings’]; Joseph.) in our lit. pass. w. act. sense fly open, spring open, open of guarded doors [οἱ κεκλισμένοι πυ]λῶνες ἐν [ὀνό]ματι θεοῦ ἀνεπετ[άσθησαν] then the gates [that had been closed] opened in the name of God AcPl Ha 3, 24.—DELG s.v. πετάννυμι.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

act. act. = active

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 70
